meta.title: SwishOS | AI Agent Security & Governance
meta.description: SwishOS secures AI agents that take real-world actions — spending money, calling tools, and touching data. Red-teaming, guardrails, and continuous evals.

hero.subtitle: SwishOS red-teams and guardrails AI agents that spend money, call tools, and touch data — so they are safe in production. Built by an architect who shipped LLM guardrails for a 5.6M-user national healthcare & financial infrastructure platform.

core.title: Red-Teaming & Testing
core.inventoryDesc: Proactive red-teaming against prompt injections, dev-mode bypasses, and unauthorized tool calls (OWASP LLM Top 10 / Agentic Top 10).

featuresPage.heroSubtitle: Three disciplines, applied in order: find out how your agent breaks, stop it breaking, then prove it stays fixed as your models and prompts change.
featuresPage.agenticDesc: Adversarial testing against your own endpoint — jailbreaks, indirect prompt injection through retrieved content, PII exfiltration, and unauthorized tool or action calls. You get reproducible payload logs, not a scanner score.
featuresPage.ledgerDesc: Input gates, output harmful-compliance detection that catches jailbreak acceptance the input filter misses, action-authorization with human-in-the-loop on high-blast-radius calls, and fail-closed defaults.
featuresPage.evalsDesc: Regression eval suites and telemetry wired into your release process, so a prompt tweak or model upgrade can't silently undo a fix. Safety becomes provable release over release.

visionPage.heroSubtitle: Every quarter, more AI systems move from answering questions to taking actions. The security model underneath them has not kept pace.
visionPage.contentTitle: Chatbot security was a content problem. Agent security is a systems problem.
visionPage.contentP1: For three years the industry has treated AI safety as text filtering — does the model say something it shouldn't. That framing was adequate while models only produced words. It stops being adequate the moment a model can call a tool, write to a database, or authorize a payment. At that point a prompt injection is no longer a bad answer; it is an unauthorized transaction, a deleted table, or data leaving your perimeter. OWASP named this LLM06, Excessive Agency, and it is the gap most AI security tooling still doesn't cover, because inspecting prompts tells you nothing about what the agent is permitted to do.
visionPage.contentP2: The controls that actually work here are not new — least privilege, authorization boundaries, blast-radius limits, audit trails, human approval on irreversible actions. They are the same controls that took decades to mature in banking and government systems, now applied to a non-deterministic caller. That is the work: bringing hard-won systems-security discipline to software that improvises. SwishOS exists to do that, and to prove it on a real agent that spends real money before asking anyone else to trust the approach.

pricingPage.faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about AI Security Engagements.

faqItems.q: What AI agent frameworks and stacks do you support?
faqItems.a: Any agent that exposes an HTTP endpoint or SDK interface. This includes custom Python/Node frameworks, LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, LLaMA-Index, or proprietary internal orchestration layers.

faqItems.q: Do we need to grant full source code access?
faqItems.a: No. We support black-box, gray-box, and white-box testing. While repository access yields deeper guardrail PR recommendations, red-teaming can be executed purely against your staging API endpoints under a signed NDA.

faqItems.q: What exact deliverables do we receive from an audit?
faqItems.a: You receive a severity-ranked OWASP LLM Threat Report, an executable red-team test suite, ready-to-merge guardrail PR configs (e.g. NeMo, Llama-Guard, or custom semantic rules), and a 60-minute executive debrief.

faqItems.q: How does the monthly retainer work?
faqItems.a: The retainer offers continuous threat sweeps on every release, guardrail maintenance as new jailbreaks emerge, and direct architecture reviews. It is rolling month-to-month with 30 days' notice and no long-term lock-in.
